nodes.title: Agent Trust and Governance
nodes.summary: Identity, authorization, policy, observability, audit, security, redress, and regulatory mechanisms for controlling agent behaviour.

nodes.title: Agentic Marketing Automation
nodes.summary: Marketing work executed by agents across customer data, campaign workflows, channels, and measurement systems.

nodes.title: Agentic Payments
nodes.summary: Payments initiated or completed by software agents under delegated authority, policy, and spending constraints.

nodes.title: Human-AI Workforce Coordination
nodes.summary: The planning, supervision, quality control, coaching, and handoff mechanisms that allocate work between people and AI agents.

nodes.title: Production Agent Economics
nodes.summary: The end-to-end cost and value of completed agent work after latency, failures, supervision, security, and integration are included.

nodes.title: Protocol Standardization
nodes.summary: Convergence on interoperable ways for agents, services, and machines to authenticate, request, and settle economic interactions.

nodes.title: System-of-Record Distribution
nodes.summary: Adoption through incumbent platforms that already control enterprise data, permissions, workflows, users, and budget ownership.

nodes.title: Verified Agent Traffic
nodes.summary: Machine-originated requests whose agent identity, authorization, purpose, and policy can be distinguished from malicious or unidentified automation.

nodes.title: Amazon Web Services
nodes.summary: A cloud infrastructure provider represented here through controls for recognizing legitimate AI-agent traffic.

nodes.title: Anthropic
nodes.summary: An AI developer partnering with FIS on a governed financial-crimes agent for banking institutions.

nodes.title: BBVA
nodes.summary: A global banking group represented in this graph as an early participant in a live AI-agent-initiated payment.

nodes.title: Cisco Webex
nodes.summary: Cisco's collaboration and contact-centre platform, represented as an enterprise distribution surface for coordinated human and AI work.

nodes.title: Cloudflare
nodes.summary: An internet infrastructure provider developing cryptographic identity and authorization controls for autonomous commerce.

nodes.title: DataDome
nodes.summary: A bot-management and agent-trust provider whose traffic report is used as an issuer-reported signal of machine-originated web activity.

nodes.title: FIS
nodes.summary: A financial-technology provider whose banking infrastructure, data, and client relationships are being used to develop governed AI-agent workflows with Anthropic.

nodes.title: Five9
nodes.summary: A contact-center platform expanding AI-agent workflows through healthcare deployments and Epic integration.

nodes.title: French Competition Authority
nodes.summary: The Autorite de la concurrence, represented through its formal opinion on competition in the AI-agent sector.

nodes.title: Google
nodes.summary: A technology platform represented in the seed graph through reported institutional backing of open agent-payment standards.

nodes.title: Mastercard
nodes.summary: A global payment network developing Agent Pay for delegated, tokenized agent transactions.

nodes.title: Ploy AI
nodes.summary: The publisher of a production-agent migration case study reporting changes in speed and cost.

nodes.title: ServiceNow
nodes.summary: An enterprise workflow platform that supplies shared data, permissions, and execution surfaces for embedded agent use cases.

nodes.title: Stripe
nodes.summary: A payment infrastructure provider represented in the seed graph through reported institutional backing of agent-payment standards.

nodes.title: SuccessKPI
nodes.summary: A workforce-engagement and contact-centre analytics provider integrating its platform with Cisco Webex Contact Center.

nodes.title: Talkspace
nodes.summary: A behavioral-health provider using an AI intake and triage workflow with clinical validation, escalation, and therapist handoff.

nodes.title: Tenon
nodes.summary: A marketing-workflow provider built on ServiceNow and represented here through its expanded AI-platform integration.

nodes.title: Visa
nodes.summary: A global payment network participating in agent-initiated transactions and agent-payment infrastructure.

nodes.title: x402 Foundation
nodes.summary: An open-protocol initiative for making payments native to internet and AI-agent workflows.

nodes.title: AWS recognizes legitimate AI-agent traffic
nodes.path: wiki/events/2026-07-14 AWS recognizes legitimate AI-agent traffic.md
nodes.summary: AWS published a production control pattern for authenticating legitimate AI-agent traffic with AWS WAF Bot Control.

evidence.label: Authenticate legitimate AI agent traffic with AWS WAF Bot Control

nodes.title: BBVA and Visa complete agent-initiated payment
nodes.path: wiki/events/2026-07-02 BBVA and Visa complete agent-initiated payment.md
nodes.summary: BBVA reported completing its first AI-agent-initiated transaction together with Visa. This is evidence of a live transaction, but not yet scaled recurring activity.

evidence.label: BBVA Completes Its First AI Agent-Initiated Transaction Together with Visa

nodes.title: Cloudflare presents Web Bot Auth for autonomous commerce
nodes.path: wiki/events/2026-06-09 Cloudflare presents Web Bot Auth for autonomous commerce.md
nodes.summary: Cloudflare presented Web Bot Auth as a way for merchants to cryptographically verify agent identity before processing transactions and bind that identity to specific transaction flows for fine-grained authorization. The cited slide named American Express, Mastercard, and Visa as payment-network adopters.

nodes.title: DataDome reports 45 percent Q2 agent-traffic growth
nodes.path: wiki/events/2026-07-16 DataDome reports 45 percent Q2 agent-traffic growth.md
nodes.summary: DataDome reported that AI-agent traffic increased 45 percent in the second quarter and associated the increase with adoption of agent-trust policies.

evidence.label: DataDome Report: AI Agent Traffic Surged 45% in Q2

nodes.title: FIS and Anthropic develop financial-crimes agent
nodes.path: wiki/events/2026-05-04 FIS and Anthropic develop financial-crimes agent.md
nodes.summary: FIS and Anthropic announced a Financial Crimes AI Agent for anti-money-laundering investigations. BMO and Amalgamated Bank were in development with the agent, while broader availability to FIS financial-institution clients was planned for the second half of 2026.

evidence.label: FIS Brings Agentic AI to Banking with Anthropic, Starting with Financial Crimes

nodes.title: Five9 expands healthcare AI-agent deployment
nodes.path: wiki/events/2025-07-31 Five9 expands healthcare AI-agent deployment.md
nodes.summary: Five9 described a leading academic health system expanding after three years of success, with Five9 and Epic integrated to streamline clinical workflows. The initial order was expected to produce more than $1.1 million in ARR.

evidence.label: Five9 launches new integration with Epic Toolbox

nodes.title: Five9 scales healthcare AI-agent commitment
nodes.path: wiki/events/2026-02-19 Five9 scales healthcare AI-agent commitment.md
nodes.summary: Five9 said a healthcare provider increased its commitment from about $6 million to more than $10 million in ARR under a three-year commitment, while using AI agents to drive cost savings across the business.

nodes.title: French competition authority issues AI-agent opinion
nodes.path: wiki/events/2026-07-17 French competition authority issues AI-agent opinion.md
nodes.summary: The French Competition Authority issued an opinion on competitive functioning in the AI-agent sector.

evidence.label: AI agents: the Autorite de la concurrence issues its opinion

nodes.title: Mastercard completes first Agent Pay transaction
nodes.path: wiki/events/2025-10-30 Mastercard completes first Agent Pay transaction.md
nodes.summary: Mastercard reported that Agent Pay completed its first agentic transaction on the network during the quarter. It said U.S. Bank and Citibank cardholders could use Agent Pay and named Walmart as a partner.

nodes.title: Mastercard enables tokenized agent transactions
nodes.path: wiki/events/2026-03-10 Mastercard enables tokenized agent transactions.md
nodes.summary: Mastercard said all U.S. banks' cards were capable of conducting an agentic transaction by the end of the prior year. It described Agent Pay as providing agents with network tokens, identity, transparency, issuer visibility, fraud, safety, and security controls.

nodes.title: Production agent migration improves cost and speed
nodes.path: wiki/events/2026-07-12 Production agent migration improves cost and speed.md
nodes.summary: Ploy published a production-agent migration case study reporting 2.2 times faster execution and 27 percent lower cost.

evidence.label: Migrating a production AI agent to GPT-5.6: 2.2x faster, 27% cheaper

nodes.title: ServiceNow agentifies customer production workflows
nodes.path: wiki/events/2026-05-04 ServiceNow agentifies customer production workflows.md
nodes.summary: ServiceNow described forward-deployed engineers agentifying workflows inside customer production environments. The cited examples were NTT Data keeping 70,000 configuration items under compliance, PayPal processing trillions of payments faster, and Robinhood supporting onboarding during acquisition integration and 26% headcount growth.

nodes.title: SuccessKPI brings WEM to Webex Contact Center
nodes.path: wiki/events/2026-07-15 SuccessKPI brings WEM to Webex Contact Center.md
nodes.summary: SuccessKPI announced workforce engagement management for Webex Contact Center, combining planning, quality management, coaching, analytics, and conversational intelligence for hybrid human and AI operations.

evidence.label: SuccessKPI Powers Next Generation Hybrid Contact Centers with WEM for Webex Cloud

nodes.title: Talkspace deploys clinically supervised AI intake workflow
nodes.path: wiki/events/2026-01-15 Talkspace deploys clinically supervised AI intake workflow.md
nodes.summary: Talkspace described an AI Agent Intake workflow with risk assessment, consent, safety and quality controls, escalation, and live therapist handoff. The company said outputs are validated by licensed clinicians within its QMS and that the workflow is HIPAA compliant.

evidence.label: Talkspace 44th Annual J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference slides

nodes.title: Talkspace measures AI behavioral triage outcomes
nodes.path: wiki/events/2025-10-30 Talkspace measures AI behavioral triage outcomes.md
nodes.summary: Talkspace reported a test in which its AI model improved identification and response to high-risk behaviors by 50%, increased therapeutic quality by 47%, and produced 3x higher user satisfaction versus the base model. The workflow retained real-time risk determination, live therapist referral, clinical oversight, and HIPAA protection.

nodes.title: Tenon expands on ServiceNow AI Platform
nodes.path: wiki/events/2026-07-15 Tenon expands on ServiceNow AI Platform.md
nodes.summary: Tenon announced expanded marketing automation on the ServiceNow AI Platform, linking marketing activity to shared customer data, workflows, and AI.

evidence.label: Tenon Expands Marketing Automation into the ServiceNow AI Platform

nodes.title: x402 Foundation expands institutional backing
nodes.path: wiki/events/2026-07-17 x402 Foundation expands institutional backing.md
nodes.summary: Secondary reporting described expanded backing for the x402 Foundation from major payment and technology companies, including Google, Visa, and Stripe.

evidence.label: New Linux Foundation project aims to make payments native to AI workflows

nodes.title: Agentic Evolution Navigator
nodes.summary: This is the entry point for evidence about AI-agent adoption, usage, economic activity, and constraints. Begin with the active theses, then follow events and concepts into their evidence.

nodes.title: AI agent operational deployments
nodes.summary: This synthesis is based on the user-supplied Quartr capture [AI agent operational deployments](https://web.quartr.com/) and its embedded links to 12 investor-call or slide records. The local PDF remains under `raw/private/` and is not published because it is a source capture rather than public-domain text.

nodes.title: Graph Dynamics
nodes.summary: The current seed graph suggests four reinforcing movements.

nodes.title: Graph Schema
nodes.summary: The graph separates dated evidence from reusable mechanisms and revisable interpretation.

nodes.title: Operational Log
nodes.summary: Created the domain glossary, maintenance contract, source ledger, seed events, concepts, theses, graph compiler, Obsidian configuration, and GitHub Pages explorer.

nodes.title: Query Guide
nodes.summary: Use deterministic traversal to collect a relevant evidence subgraph, then ask Codex to synthesize it.

nodes.title: Agent payments are moving from protocol to production
nodes.path: wiki/theses/Agent payments are moving from protocol to production.md
nodes.summary: Open standards and institutional backing are being joined by early live transactions. The thesis remains medium-confidence because evidence of repeated usage, merchant breadth, dispute handling, and economic value is still limited.

nodes.title: Agent-originated traffic is becoming an addressable market
nodes.path: wiki/theses/Agent-originated traffic is becoming an addressable market.md
nodes.summary: Web infrastructure is beginning to distinguish legitimate agents from undifferentiated bots, creating a basis for access policy, attribution, paid APIs, transaction fees, and agent-specific service tiers.

nodes.title: Incumbent platforms are the distribution channel
nodes.path: wiki/theses/Incumbent platforms are the distribution channel.md
nodes.summary: Enterprise agents are increasingly entering work through platforms that already hold data, permissions, workflows, and customer relationships. Native platform integrations may therefore distribute agents faster than standalone destinations.

nodes.title: Trust infrastructure monetizes before full autonomy
nodes.path: wiki/theses/Trust infrastructure monetizes before full autonomy.md
nodes.summary: Identity, traffic classification, policy, observability, security, and regulatory assurance may generate revenue before highly autonomous agents are widely trusted with consequential work.
